user_story: As an HR admin, I want to enable work from home based settings, so that employees can request and be governed by WFH policies
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on the Work From Home Settings screen; When I set "Do you support work from home based settings?" to Yes and Save; Then WFH settings are enabled

user_story: As an HR admin, I want to add a new work from home settings template, so that WFH rules can be tailored by employee class
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on the Work From Home Settings screen; When I click Add new work from home and define a template name, employee class specificity and max instances per month; Then the settings template is saved and listed

user_story: As an HR admin, I want to set the maximum number of work from home instances per month, so that WFH usage stays within limits
acceptance_criteria: Given I am configuring a WFH settings template; When I enter the max number of work from home instances per month; Then the limit is enforced for employees

user_story: As an HR admin, I want to view and edit WFH settings templates with their class specificity and monthly limits, so that I can keep WFH policies accurate
acceptance_criteria: Given WFH settings templates exist; When I open the Work From Home Settings screen and click View or Edit; Then each template shows its name, class specific flag, max instances and applicability and my edits are saved

user_story: As an HR admin, I want to save and continue to the next configuration step, so that I can complete the attendance setup flow
acceptance_criteria: Given I have configured WFH settings; When I click Save & Next; Then the settings are saved and I proceed to the next screen
